description: Ground truth for the web-uplift agentic-audit eval. This fixture is a FROZEN copy of the seeded-issue playground: its issues must not be fixed, because they are the eval's ground truth. A correct audit of site/ (served as-is, default 'issue' mode) must surface all nine findings below with the right principle and a sensible Modern Web Guidance id. The live playground/ in the repo is the genuinely-fixed product and must report ZERO of these (see eval/README.md). The first six are the seeded CSS scenarios (one per scenario route); the last three are page-level Lighthouse-dimension findings the agentic audit surfaces by choosing to run Lighthouse + axe. Principle ids use the current knowledge/principles.json ids (re-baselined 2026-06-13 for the expanded 15-principle set: F-007's principleId moved be-accessible -> be-inclusive after the rename). See PLAN.md open question #2 on scoring an agentic eval.
rebaseline2026_06_13: The principle set grew from 9 to 15 (docs/principles-analysis.md, adopted). We re-ran the agentic audit against this fixture with the wider principles and verified whether any net-new principle legitimately surfaces a NEW fixture-only finding. It does NOT: the only new-principle observations on the served site (no Content-Security-Policy header; a client-rendered #nav/#view shell that is blank without JS) are properties of the bare `npx serve` static host and the demo harness, and they are present IDENTICALLY on the genuinely-fixed live playground/. Counting them as fixture findings would be dishonest (they are not seeded differences and would also fire on the 'clean' playground, breaking the precision guard). The correct treatment, per the guard-criteria / 'quality without shaming' design, is to report be-private-and-secure as not-applicable (judged: localhost demo, no real transport/headers to set) and be-resilient/be-internationalised/be-sustainable/be-agent-ready as not-applicable or opted-out on BOTH subjects (see expectedPrincipleOutcomes and the repo web-uplift.json). So the seeded ground truth stays at nine; the rename is the only change. The new findings F-007 principleId is now be-inclusive.

expectedPrincipleOutcomes.note: Applicability the audit should report for the expanded principles on this fixture (issue mode). default-expectation principles are judged for real; contextual ones that legitimately do not apply to a localhost CSS-demo are not-applicable (judged) and must NOT be counted as issues. This same shape is expected on the live playground, where the nine findings below all become pass.
pass-or-issues: be-trustworthy (pass: buttons are demo-only, no forms/dark patterns)
not-applicable-or-opted-out: be-private-and-secure (not-applicable, judged: bare localhost static host; no transport/headers/auth surface to assess. NOT a fixture finding because the live playground shares the identical serving setup.)
be-resilient (not-applicable/opted-out: a deliberately client-rendered CSS-scenario demo; offline/installable out of scope and the no-JS shell is a shared harness property, not a seeded issue)
be-internationalised (not-applicable, judged: single-locale English demo with no locale-sensitive data)
be-sustainable (not-applicable/judged proportionally: a tiny hand-authored demo, weight already minimal)
be-agent-ready (opted-out: static UX demo with no agent-facing surface)

scenarios.detectableVia: Emulate prefers-color-scheme: dark; the .ndm-card background stays rgb(255,255,255) with no light-dark() declared.
scenarios.fix: Declare color-scheme: light dark and use light-dark() for surface and text colors so the card follows the user preference. Guidance id: dark-mode.

scenarios.expectedGuidanceQuery: respect reduced motion preference accessible animation
scenarios.detectableVia: Emulate prefers-reduced-motion: reduce; getAnimations() on .mv-card returns 1 running animation (mv-slide).
scenarios.fix: Gate the animation behind @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference) so it only runs when the user has not requested reduced motion.

scenarios.expectedGuidanceQuery: responsive layout adapt to viewport mobile
scenarios.detectableVia: Resize to viewport 360x800; issue mode produces ~1158px of horizontal overflow and clips .fl-hero (width: 1200px).
scenarios.fix: Replace fixed width with width: 100%; max-width: 1200px and box-sizing: border-box so the layout adapts to narrow viewports.

scenarios.expectedGuidanceQuery: visible focus indicator focus-visible keyboard accessibility
scenarios.detectableVia: Focus a .pf-btn control; issue mode sets outline: none with no :focus-visible replacement, so the computed outline-style is none.
scenarios.fix: Remove the blanket outline: none and add a :focus-visible outline so keyboard focus is clearly indicated without affecting pointer clicks.

scenarios.expectedGuidanceQuery: prevent layout shift reserve space CLS aspect-ratio
scenarios.detectableVia: The layout-shift observer records CLS as the paragraph below .ls-slot shifts down ~600ms after load when the banner injects; issue mode reserves no space.
scenarios.fix: Reserve the banner's space up front with min-height (or aspect-ratio) on the slot so the late content does not shift surrounding layout.

scenarios.detectableVia: The same .cq-card stays flex-direction: row inside the 240px .cq-narrow container; the component does not respond to its container width (no container-type / @container).
scenarios.fix: Add container-type: inline-size to the wrapper and a @container (max-width: 320px) rule that stacks the card, so the component adapts to its container rather than the viewport.

scenarios.detectableVia: Lighthouse accessibility flags color-contrast; axe-core (injected via the evaluate primitive) reports a serious color-contrast violation on the three .pf-btn buttons (grey #f2f2f2 background, low-contrast label).
scenarios.fix: Raise button text/background contrast to at least 4.5:1; re-verify with axe / Lighthouse.

scenarios.detectableVia: Lighthouse SEO reports meta-description missing; a DOM probe confirms a <title> but no <meta name="description">.
scenarios.fix: Add a concise <meta name="description"> to the head.

scenarios.detectableVia: Lighthouse best-practices reports errors-in-console with one 404 (a missing resource, e.g. the favicon, under the bare static server).
scenarios.fix: Add the missing resource (e.g. a favicon) or remove the reference so the page loads without console errors.

falsePositiveCheck: The site/ fixture also ships ?mode=fixed (the corrected stylesheets). An audit of ?mode=fixed must report zero of the six CSS scenarios. The page-level findings F-007..F-009 are document-level and are corrected in the genuinely-fixed live playground/, not toggled here.
